notes: M3 spectrogram/waterfall session manifest (PLAN.md M3: single manifest instead of one-file-per-signal -- deliberate deviation, noted in PROGRESS.md). Keyed by the exact <id> in every `spectro <id>` line across game/story/scripts/act1/*.scene. Parsed by WaterfallSessionLibrary, consumed by WaterfallSession (game/scripts/systems/). Fake physics throughout: authored numbers, no FFT. T1's canon frequency is 1421.6 MHz, drift -0.11 Hz/s, beam 2 only (story/secret-bible.md IX) -- cal_pulsar deliberately uses a different frequency (1400.0 MHz) since it is mundane calibration work, not T1; its drift_mhz_s is a gameplay-scale teaching drift, not the canon number (which is invisible at MHz scale). signal_beam is only a live gate for the beam_scan kind (the 'beam 2 only' discovery); on the other T1 sessions it is canon annotation. cell_seconds drives a visible, countable on/off cadence (canon 11-minute cells, bible D2-S5); 0 = steady trace. events open timed payload windows: kind shape_burst = broadband smear on the waterfall; kind decode_text (with text_key or text_source:'observer_name') = readout line -- observer_name is a RESERVED capability, unauthored (canon lock IX limits where the typed name may appear). answer_available is deliberately OMITTED on every Act 1 session: Day 5's transgression answer is authored as a dialogue choice in d5_observatory.scene (setflag answered_signal, read by d7_observatory.scene). The answer_available capability is reserved for a future console-native ANSWER verb.
